item.author: The Verge <subscriptions@theverge.com>
item.description: Apple Computer Company was founded on April 1st, 1976, and is now celebrating its 50th anniversary as one of the world's most valuable companies. Over five decades, Apple has shaped technology and culture through iconic products like the iPad, iPhone, iPod, and MacBook, while experiencing both triumphs and failures across different eras of leadership.
item.keywords: Apple 50th anniversary, Apple history, Steve Jobs, iconic Apple products, tech industry impact

item.title: The Fall in the House of Sora [8/8]
item.author: "Spyglass Afterthoughts..." <mgsiegler@substack.com>
item.description: Apple App Store review processes are becoming overwhelmed by a surge in app submissions, creating bottlenecks in the update pipeline. The review system cannot scale with submission rates, creating a systemic problem with no clear resolution.
item.keywords: App Store review process, app submission backlog, scaling challenges, app updates

item.title: The Fall in the House of Sora [7/8]
item.author: "Spyglass Afterthoughts..." <mgsiegler@substack.com>
item.description: Apple is offering stock options to employees in an effort to stem talent losses to OpenAI and Meta, indicating growing competition for AI engineering talent among major tech companies.
item.keywords: Apple talent retention, stock options, OpenAI recruitment, tech talent competition

item.title: The Fall in the House of Sora [6/8]
item.author: "Spyglass Afterthoughts..." <mgsiegler@substack.com>
item.description: China's AI talent gap—not just model performance—may be the critical competitive issue going forward. While focus has centered on Chinese AI models, the underlying talent shortage could be a larger structural challenge in the long term.
item.keywords: China AI talent, AI competition, talent gap, competitive advantage

item.title: The Fall in the House of Sora [5/8]
item.author: "Spyglass Afterthoughts..." <mgsiegler@substack.com>
item.description: Internal conflict between Dario and Daniella Amodei versus Sam Altman and Greg Brockman at OpenAI led to the creation of Anthropic, with roots tracing back a decade. The schism involved egos, strategic disagreements, and double-dealing that spiraled into a major corporate rift. Both companies are now valued in the hundreds of billions and racing toward public offerings while competing in AI development.
item.keywords: OpenAI Anthropic rivalry, Dario Amodei, Sam Altman, internal conflict origins, IPO preparation

item.title: The Fall in the House of Sora [4/8]
item.author: "Spyglass Afterthoughts..." <mgsiegler@substack.com>
item.description: Project Hail Mary surpassed $200M globally ($137M domestically) in two weeks with a strong 34% second weekend box office drop. It became Amazon MGM Studios' most successful film ever, outperforming Creed III. Sequel discussions are already underway with author Andy Weir, and the film's success provides momentum for Amazon's upcoming Bond film project.
item.keywords: Project Hail Mary box office, $200M global revenue, Amazon MGM Studios, film sequel, Andy Weir

item.title: The Fall in the House of Sora [3/8]
item.author: "Spyglass Afterthoughts..." <mgsiegler@substack.com>
item.description: All original xAI co-founders except Elon Musk have departed, with Ross Nordeen being the latest to leave. This represents a common pattern in AI labs where founding teams disperse. Despite Nordeen's deep ties to Musk (Tesla, Twitter acquisition), the talent exodus highlights that investments were bets on Musk's vision rather than the team itself. xAI has received SpaceX shares as compensation but faces pressure to deliver results.
item.keywords: xAI co-founders departure, Ross Nordeen, Elon Musk, AI lab talent turnover, SpaceX equity compensation

item.title: The Fall in the House of Sora [2/8]
item.author: "Spyglass Afterthoughts..." <mgsiegler@substack.com>
item.description: ChatGPT's app store continues to struggle with adoption, repeating the failure of the custom GPTs directory from two years prior. Users find it faster and easier to use native apps and websites directly—booking through Booking.com or Uber's app—rather than through ChatGPT. Enterprise integrations and potential future device ecosystems remain the main strategic rationale, though past device efforts by Amazon have also underperformed.
item.keywords: ChatGPT apps, app store adoption, custom GPTs, enterprise integration, OpenAI devices

item.title: The Fall in the House of Sora [1/8]
item.author: "Spyglass Afterthoughts..." <mgsiegler@substack.com>
item.description: OpenAI has discontinued its Sora video generation product, ending a major push into video AI. The service burned $1M per day and required separate model training distinct from their LLM efforts. Key personnel disputes with Meta over talent hiring and the focus needed for their 'Spud' model contributed to the decision. The discontinuation was poorly communicated to Disney, who received less than an hour's notice before the plug was pulled.
item.keywords: OpenAI Sora shutdown, video generation AI, compute resource allocation, Disney partnership, AI model training costs

item.title: ☕ Event Travel Brew [9/9]
item.author: Morning Brew <crew@morningbrew.com>
item.description: Multiple major events scheduled for 2026 include: Mexico's San Marcos Fair (April 18-May 3), FIFA World Cup (June 11-July 19 across US/Canada/Mexico with US games on July 4), Edinburgh Festival Fringe (August 7-31), Munich Oktoberfest (Sept 19-Oct 4), Thailand lantern festivals (Nov 24-25), and Sydney New Year's Eve celebrations.
item.keywords: 2026 events, FIFA World Cup 2026, Oktoberfest, Edinburgh Festival, global events calendar

item.title: ☕ Event Travel Brew [8/9]
item.author: Morning Brew <crew@morningbrew.com>
item.description: The Feast of the Black Nazarene in Manila, Philippines attracts almost 10 million devotees annually for a 30-hour procession featuring a replica of a 17th-century wooden statue of Jesus. Hasidic Jews gather in Uman, Ukraine for Rosh Hashanah with up to 60,000 believers attending the pilgrimage at Rabbi Nachman's tomb, even continuing after Russia's 2022 invasion.
item.keywords: Feast of Black Nazarene, Manila pilgrimage, Uman Ukraine, Rosh Hashanah, religious sites

item.title: ☕ Event Travel Brew [7/9]
item.author: Morning Brew <crew@morningbrew.com>
item.description: Religious pilgrimages drive massive global travel and economic activity, generating nearly $290 billion in economic activity as of 2024. The 2025 Hajj in Saudi Arabia drew over 1.6 million pilgrims paying $5,000-$25,000 per person. The Maha Kumbh Mela festival drew over 660 million people last year, making it the world's largest human gathering.
item.keywords: religious pilgrimage, Hajj, Maha Kumbh Mela, religious tourism, economic impact

item.title: ☕ Event Travel Brew [6/9]
item.author: Morning Brew <crew@morningbrew.com>
item.description: The Sturgis Motorcycle Rally in South Dakota attracts approximately 500,000 people annually to a town of 7,000 residents. The 2025 rally generated about $1.6 million in state and local taxes, up 13% from 2024. However, rally expenses have consistently exceeded revenue in recent years.
item.keywords: Sturgis Motorcycle Rally, South Dakota tourism, event revenue, local taxes

item.title: ☕ Event Travel Brew [5/9]
item.author: Morning Brew <crew@morningbrew.com>
item.description: F1 promoter fees accounted for approximately $824 million of F1's 2025 earnings. The Belgian Grand Prix contributes around $250 million annually to Belgium's economy. However, cancellation of two Middle Eastern races (Bahrain and Saudi Arabia) due to Iran conflict could cost F1 about $200 million in revenue.
item.keywords: F1 promoter fees, race cancellation, Belgium Grand Prix, Middle East races

item.title: ☕ Event Travel Brew [4/9]
item.author: Morning Brew <crew@morningbrew.com>
item.description: Formula 1 generated $3.9 billion in revenue in 2025, a 14% increase from 2024. The 2025 season was the most-attended F1 season ever with 6.7 million spectators across 24 races, 19 events sold out, 11 races set attendance records, and the British Grand Prix drew 500,000 fans.
item.keywords: Formula 1 revenue, F1 attendance records, motorsports tourism, British Grand Prix

item.title: ☕ Event Travel Brew [3/9]
item.author: Morning Brew <crew@morningbrew.com>
item.description: California will host multiple major sporting events generating billions in local revenue: Super Bowl and NBA All-Star Game already occurred, FIFA World Cup games coming summer 2026, another Super Bowl in 2027, and Olympics in 2028. The 49ers CEO claimed the recent Super Bowl brought ~$500 million to the Bay Area.
item.keywords: California events, Super Bowl, FIFA World Cup, Olympics 2028, sports tourism revenue

item.title: ☕ Event Travel Brew [2/9]
item.author: Morning Brew <crew@morningbrew.com>
item.description: BTS announced a reunion international tour, causing Booking.com searches to surge 6,700% for a November tour date in Taiwan. Flights and hotel rooms in 34 cities around the world sold out quickly following the announcement.
item.keywords: BTS reunion tour, K-pop, event travel, booking surge, music tour

item.title: ☕ Event Travel Brew [1/9]
item.author: Morning Brew <crew@morningbrew.com>
item.description: Event travel is driving significant economic growth globally. Music tourism could reach $9 billion by 2030 (50% increase from 2023), while sports tourism is projected to exceed $1 trillion in coming years. Taylor Swift's and Beyoncé's tours demonstrated massive economic benefits for host cities through restaurant and hotel revenue.
item.keywords: event travel, music tourism, sports tourism, economic impact, concert tours

item.title: DCB #194｜The CCP’s latest “Ethnic Unity Law” creates ethnic division and confrontation. [3/3]
item.author: Dialogue China <dc8964@97296837.mailchimpapp.com>
item.description: During China's 2026 'Two Sessions,' rural elderly pension support emerged as a key issue. NPC deputy Lei Maoduan proposed raising basic pensions for farmers aged 70 and above to 500 yuan monthly over three years, addressing the current inadequate payment of just over 100 yuan per month. Scholars suggest combining higher age-based subsidies with strengthened social safety net support for vulnerable rural elderly, particularly the oldest-old demographic.
item.keywords: rural pensions, elderly care China, pension reform 2026, social welfare, aging population

item.title: DCB #194｜The CCP’s latest “Ethnic Unity Law” creates ethnic division and confrontation. [2/3]
item.author: Dialogue China <dc8964@97296837.mailchimpapp.com>
item.description: The European Union introduced the 'Industrial Accelerator Act' imposing higher foreign investment thresholds in four sectors: batteries, electric vehicles, photovoltaics, and critical raw materials. The policy uses technology transfer requirements, equity restrictions, and localization mandates to reshape market rules under 'EU manufacturing first' principle. For Chinese companies, this shifts competition from price-based to institutional compliance thresholds, forcing deeper localization while benefiting firms already established in Europe.
item.keywords: EU Industrial Accelerator Act, foreign investment restrictions, green manufacturing, supply chain localization, China trade barriers

item.title: DCB #194｜The CCP’s latest “Ethnic Unity Law” creates ethnic division and confrontation. [1/3]
item.author: Dialogue China <dc8964@97296837.mailchimpapp.com>
item.description: China's newly adopted 'Law on Promoting Ethnic Unity and Progress' is criticized as a tool for forced ethnic assimilation rather than genuine unity. The law shifts ethnic policy from administrative measures to legal enforcement, introducing four problematic provisions: replacing bilingual education with Chinese-language supremacy (Articles 15, 22), forcing cultural identity through law (Article 3's 'five identifications'), promoting compulsory mixed residential integration (Article 28), and enabling extraterritorial enforcement against overseas critics (Article 64). Critics argue these rigid legal frameworks will intensify ethnic conflict rather than promote stability.
item.keywords: China ethnic policy, Ethnic Unity Law 2026, linguistic assimilation, minority rights, cultural identity enforcement

item.title: DCB #194｜中共最新《民族團結法》實為製造民族分裂與對立 [6/6]
item.author: Dialogue China <dc8964@97296837.mailchimpapp.com>
item.description: China's 2026 GDP growth target of 4.5-5% represents a deliberate policy choice rather than a growth slowdown, providing flexibility for structural adjustment, risk prevention, and external shocks. The target maintains strong global positioning while shifting emphasis from speed to quality, incorporating employment, price stability, and livelihood improvements. The range allows regional differentiation while maintaining growth as employment foundation, embodying a 'worst-case preparation, best-case effort' approach.
item.keywords: GDP growth target 2026, economic policy flexibility, high-quality development, structural adjustment, regional differentiation, employment stability, macroeconomic management

item.title: DCB #194｜中共最新《民族團結法》實為製造民族分裂與對立 [5/6]
item.author: Dialogue China <dc8964@97296837.mailchimpapp.com>
item.description: China's food delivery subsidy wars, involving billions in platform spending, have failed to create genuine prosperity despite short-term traffic stimulus. The result is across-the-board value destruction: restaurants face increased volume without profit gains and accelerating closures; suppliers experience forced price cuts; consumers will eventually pay higher prices and face quality decline and food safety risks. The subsidy competition exemplifies market failure where cross-business profit leverage creates a prisoners' dilemma, eroding profitability and reinforcing deflationary expectations.
item.keywords: delivery platform subsidies, anti-monopoly regulation, market distortion, restaurant profitability, supply chain compression, consumer risk, platform competition

item.title: DCB #194｜中共最新《民族團結法》實為製造民族分裂與對立 [4/6]
item.author: Dialogue China <dc8964@97296837.mailchimpapp.com>
item.description: The 2026 National People's Congress highlighted unprecedented public focus on raising farmer pension payments, though the actual increase of only 20 yuan disappointed expectations. Despite limited policy breakthrough, the incident represents significant shifts in public discourse: multiple representatives vocalized support, mainstream media unusually criticized the measure as insufficient, and social consensus on the need for substantial increases (300-1000 yuan range within 3-5 years) has solidified, creating a positive feedback loop between media, experts, and policy discussions.
item.keywords: farmer pension increase, public discourse, Two Sessions 2026, social consensus, media coverage, welfare expansion, income distribution

item.title: DCB #194｜中共最新《民族團結法》實為製造民族分裂與對立 [3/6]
item.author: Dialogue China <dc8964@97296837.mailchimpapp.com>
item.description: Rural pension security in China faces significant challenges as elderly farmers, particularly those over 70, receive only 100+ yuan monthly, forcing many to continue high-intensity labor. A National People's Congress representative proposed raising basic pension to 500 yuan monthly over three years. The underlying issue is systemic: rural pensions depend on government subsidies with low absolute amounts and slow growth, creating widening gaps with urban pensions and constraining consumption and risk resilience.
item.keywords: rural pension reform, elderly welfare, basic pension 500 yuan, urban-rural disparity, social security, aging population, 2026 Two Sessions

item.title: DCB #194｜中共最新《民族團結法》實為製造民族分裂與對立 [2/6]
item.author: Dialogue China <dc8964@97296837.mailchimpapp.com>
item.description: The EU's Industrial Accelerator Act introduces technology transfer restrictions, equity limitations, and localization requirements for foreign investment in batteries, electric vehicles, photovoltaics, and critical raw materials. The policy reshapes market rules through public procurement integration and supply chain security measures, attempting to make low-carbon manufacturing commercially viable in Europe while reorienting global industrial division of labor.
item.keywords: EU Industrial Accelerator Act, foreign direct investment restrictions, technology transfer requirements, localization mandates, green manufacturing, supply chain security, China export competitiveness

item.title: DCB #194｜中共最新《民族團結法》實為製造民族分裂與對立 [1/6]
item.author: Dialogue China <dc8964@97296837.mailchimpapp.com>
item.description: China's new Ethnic Unity and Progress Promotion Law (民族團結進步促進法), passed at the March 2026 National People's Congress, is analyzed as effectively a forced assimilation law rather than a genuine unity measure. The law establishes four major problematic mechanisms: replacing bilingual policies with Han Chinese language dominance in education and public spaces; embedding political ideology (including CCP loyalty) into legal requirements with vague enforcement boundaries; forcing mixed residential patterns between Han and ethnic minorities; and granting cross-border enforcement authority against foreign individuals or organizations critical of China's ethnic policies.
item.keywords: Ethnic Unity Law 2026, Han language dominance, forced assimilation, ethnic minority rights, China National People's Congress, cultural autonomy, cross-border enforcement

item.description: Spade raised $40 million for fintech tools focused on smarter bank transaction data analysis.
item.keywords: Spade, fintech, $40 million funding, transaction data, banking

item.title: Inside The Startup Making Chemicals From Sugar Instead Of Fossil Fuels [3/11]
item.author: This Week at YC <info@ycombinator.com>
item.description: Glimpse, a YC company, raised $35 million in Series A funding to bring AI-native infrastructure to consumer packaged goods (CPG) and retail sectors.
item.keywords: Glimpse, Series A funding, $35 million, AI infrastructure, CPG, retail

item.title: Inside The Startup Making Chemicals From Sugar Instead Of Fossil Fuels [2/11]
item.author: This Week at YC <info@ycombinator.com>
item.description: François Chollet discusses a different approach to AGI beyond scaling, focusing on understanding intelligence from first principles. He launched ARC V3, a new benchmark designed to measure the ability to learn, adapt, and reason efficiently in entirely new environments, arguing that current systems may be hitting limits and true general intelligence may require fundamentally different approaches.
item.keywords: François Chollet, AGI, artificial general intelligence, ARC prize, AI benchmarks, deep learning

item.title: Inside The Startup Making Chemicals From Sugar Instead Of Fossil Fuels [1/11]
item.author: This Week at YC <info@ycombinator.com>
item.description: Solugen, a YC company, is reinventing chemical manufacturing by using biology and chemistry to produce chemicals from sugar instead of fossil fuels. Co-founders Gaurab Chakrabarti and Sean Hunt grew the company from a $7,000 PVC reactor to a billion-dollar valuation while competing with industry giants in the $6 trillion chemical manufacturing industry. Their approach combines enzymatic and catalytic production methods.
item.keywords: Solugen, chemical manufacturing, bio-based chemicals, fossil fuel alternatives, enzymatic production

item.title: 【住加男人】 點解要搞眾籌？由 GoFundMe 到 Kickstarter 的災難與掙扎 😱 ｜JM9
item.author: 住加男人－CanMen <bingo@patreon.com>
item.description: A newsletter post titled '【住加男人】 點解要搞眾籌？由 GoFundMe 到 Kickstarter 的災難與掙扎' (Why Do Crowdfunding? From GoFundMe to Kickstarter Disasters and Struggles) explores the challenges and issues associated with crowdfunding platforms including GoFundMe and Kickstarter. The post appears to discuss why people resort to crowdfunding and the problems they encounter on these platforms.
item.keywords: crowdfunding, GoFundMe, Kickstarter, fundraising challenges, crowdfunding disasters

item.title: ☕ Coming correct [7/13]
item.author: Morning Brew <crew@morningbrew.com>
item.description: New Disney CEO Josh D'Amaro's first week and a half has been marked by three major setbacks: a domestic violence scandal involving The Bachelorette star Taylor Frankie Paul derailed the new ABC season within 24 hours of his appointment, OpenAI ended its planned three-year $1 billion partnership with Disney involving the Sora AI video-generation app, and Epic Games announced 1,000+ job cuts citing declining Fortnite engagement.
item.keywords: Disney, Josh D'Amaro, CEO, OpenAI Sora, Epic Games, Fortnite, AI partnership

item.title: ☕ Coming correct [6/13]
item.author: Morning Brew <crew@morningbrew.com>
item.description: The Treasury Department announced that President Trump will become the first sitting U.S. president to have his signature appear on paper currency, breaking with tradition where only the Treasury Secretary and Treasurer signatures appear. The department is also planning a controversial commemorative coin featuring Trump's image for America's 250th anniversary, despite federal law prohibiting living presidents' images on U.S. currency.
item.keywords: Trump, Treasury Department, currency signature, paper money, commemorative coin, 250th anniversary

item.title: ☕ Coming correct [5/13]
item.author: Morning Brew <crew@morningbrew.com>
item.description: House Republican leaders rejected a Senate compromise bill that would fund most of the Department of Homeland Security while excluding Immigration and Customs Enforcement and Border Patrol, instead backing a short-term funding measure for all of DHS. This decision is likely to extend the partial government shutdown, though the DHS announced TSA employees will start receiving paychecks as soon as Monday under a presidential executive order.
item.keywords: government shutdown, DHS funding, House Republicans, Senate, TSA, ICE, Border Patrol

item.title: ☕ Coming correct [4/13]
item.author: Morning Brew <crew@morningbrew.com>
item.description: Following rising oil prices amid the Iran war, market traders now predict a 52% probability of a Federal Reserve rate hike in 2026, according to CME Group FedWatch tool data reported by CNBC. This represents a dramatic reversal from one month prior when traders expected rate cuts, with CME data showing only 20% odds of a rate hike by September despite 90% odds for a rate cut just last month.
item.keywords: Federal Reserve, rate hike, inflation, oil prices, CME FedWatch, monetary policy

item.title: ☕ Coming correct [3/13]
item.author: Morning Brew <crew@morningbrew.com>
item.description: President Trump extended the deadline for peace negotiations with Iran by 10 days on Thursday, but markets did not rebound as expected under the so-called TACO trade (Trump Always Chickens Out). Crude oil prices surged to $110 per barrel as Israel threatened escalation and Iran remained resistant to peace talks, dampening the typical market recovery effect.
item.keywords: Trump, Iran negotiations, TACO trade, crude oil, $110 per barrel, geopolitical

item.title: ☕ Coming correct [2/13]
item.author: Morning Brew <crew@morningbrew.com>
item.description: Tech giants have suffered significant declines since October 2025, with Microsoft down 34% and Meta down 29% from peaks, while the Nasdaq has dropped 11% from its October high. Even Nvidia, the AI chipmaker, is down nearly 20% from its October peak. Microsoft is experiencing its worst quarter since the 2008 financial crisis as investors worry about AI infrastructure spending and competition from startups like OpenAI and Anthropic.
item.keywords: Microsoft, Meta, Nvidia, tech stocks, AI spending, correction, October 2025

item.title: ☕ Coming correct [1/13]
item.author: Morning Brew <crew@morningbrew.com>
item.description: Major stock indices fell sharply on March 27, 2026, with the Dow and S&P 500 entering correction territory amid geopolitical tensions over a potential prolonged war in Iran and rising energy prices. The Nasdaq ended its fifth consecutive down week—its worst losing streak since 2022—with tech stocks particularly hard hit, down nearly their worst week in a year. The 10-year Treasury yield rose to 4.440% and Bitcoin fell 4.19% to $65,969.61.
item.keywords: stock market decline, correction territory, Iran war, tech stocks, Nasdaq, S&P 500, Dow Jones

item.title: 周報 ︳靈活
item.author: 獨立媒體 inmediahk.net <bingo@patreon.com>
item.description: 獨立媒體採訪宏福苑「獨立委員會」聽證會。由於委員會限制每家媒體只能派一名記者進場，但會議內容信息量巨大，媒體需要動員多名記者。獨立媒體和其他媒體通過登記公眾席讓更多記者進場，並安排記者在場外採訪出席聽證會的人士。
item.keywords: 宏福苑獨立委員會, 聽證會採訪, 獨立媒體報導, 記者限制政策, 現場採訪

item.title: Rep. Letlow Bought DRS. Then Two Pentagon Contracts Followed. [2/6]
item.author: Quiver Quantitative <info@quiverquant.com>
item.description: Rep. Letlow has 210+ late STOCK Act disclosures worth up to $3.3M, and the House Ethics Committee waived all fines associated with these late filings. Letlow sits on the Appropriations Subcommittee on National Security, which votes on the defense budget that funds contracts like those awarded to DRS.
item.keywords: STOCK Act disclosures, House Ethics Committee, late filings, Appropriations Committee, defense budget

item.title: Rep. Letlow Bought DRS. Then Two Pentagon Contracts Followed. [1/6]
item.author: Quiver Quantitative <info@quiverquant.com>
item.description: Rep. Julia Letlow (R-LA) purchased Leonardo DRS stock on February 2nd, and weeks later DRS was selected for two major Pentagon contract vehicles: the SHIELD missile defense IDIQ with a $151B ceiling and the ATSP5 rapid tech program with a $25B ceiling (shared among 13 contractors). Letlow is the only member of Congress who traded DRS stock. Her office states the trades were broker-managed by Merrill Lynch. DRS stock has risen 14% since her purchase.
item.keywords: Rep. Julia Letlow, Leonardo DRS, Pentagon contracts, SHIELD missile defense, ATSP5 program, congressional trading

item.title: ☕️ Miami exodus [10/13]
item.author: Morning Brew <crew@morningbrew.com>
item.description: Northern Kentucky farmers Ida Huddleston and her daughter Delsia Bare rejected a $26 million offer from an anonymous AI company to buy land for a proposed data center—approximately 10 times the local land value. They cited their family's farming heritage through the Great Depression, stating the money 'doesn't mean anything.' The company has revised its plan and could still build nearby.
item.keywords: data center, farmland, AI company, Kentucky, $26 million offer

item.title: ☕️ Miami exodus [9/13]
item.author: Morning Brew <crew@morningbrew.com>
item.description: DraftKings launched DK Replay, a new in-app feature allowing users to bet on simulated pitch outcomes from historical MLB games. Users have 15 seconds to bet on whether a pitch is a ball, strike, or in play, with anonymized players shown by statistical ratings. The feature is currently only available in Oregon but DraftKings plans to expand it.
item.keywords: DraftKings, sports betting, historical games, DK Replay, MLB
